title: Beyond Good and Evil
author: Nietzsche, Friedrich Wilhelm
summary: Nietzsche attacks philosophical dogmatism and the unquestioned reverence for "truth," personifying Truth as a woman who resists crude wooing. He diagnoses metaphysicians' prejudice in believing high values require pure origins, indicting Plato and Christian-Platonism as massive errors that inverted life by denying perspective. He urges suspicion of antitheses of value, asks whether untruth and illusion might be life-preserving, and calls for new philosophers who embrace dangerous "perhapses." He exposes philosophers as instinct-driven advocates defending prejudices as truths, ridicules Kantian and Spinozist pieties, and celebrates free European spirits.

characters.name: Truth (personified)
characters.personality: elusive, resistant to crude dogmatism; likened to a woman who cannot be won by force
characters.lifespan: remains unconquered; resists dogmatic philosophers in the narrative

characters.name: Philosophers (general)
characters.personality: portrayed as dogmatic, instinct-driven, pretentious defenders of prejudices called truths
characters.lifespan: subject to critique and exposure; urged to be superseded by new kinds of thinkers

characters.name: Dogmatists
characters.personality: solemn, conclusive, and certain; enamored of absolute foundations
characters.lifespan: depicted as declining, caricatured, and at risk of collapse

characters.name: Plato
characters.personality: blamed for inventing Pure Spirit and the Good in Itself; source of dangerous inversion
characters.lifespan: his doctrine is portrayed as a malady to be overcome

characters.name: Socrates
characters.personality: mentioned as possible corrupter in a provocative, ironic way
characters.lifespan: invoked in historical critique (his hemlock debated) within the argument

characters.name: Jesuits (as group)
characters.personality: example of a force that attempted to unbend European tension; conservative and disciplinary
characters.lifespan: represented as one historical attempt to redirect European spirit

characters.name: Democrats / democratic enlightenment
characters.personality: another historical attempt to ease cultural tension via press and popular education
characters.lifespan: portrayed as an alternative remedy to Jesuitism, with limited success

characters.name: Germans
characters.personality: credited with inventing gunpowder and printing; caricatured as square-makers
characters.lifespan: mentioned as a cultural force in Europe's history

characters.name: Good Europeans / Free Spirits (collective)
characters.personality: awake, wakeful, tense, and poised to aim at new goals; self-conscious critics of dogma
characters.lifespan: presented as heirs of struggle against Platonic-Christian errors and as agents of future philosophy

characters.name: Oedipus
characters.personality: used metaphorically as solver of riddles (rendezvous of questions)
characters.lifespan: serves as an interrogative figure in Nietzsche's allegory

characters.name: Sphinx (mythic)
characters.personality: poses questions; symbolic catalyst for intellectual peril and inquiry
characters.lifespan: remains a testing challenge for philosophers in the text

characters.name: Metaphysicians (group)
characters.personality: committed to antitheses of value and traditional valuations; resistant to doubt
characters.lifespan: their foundational beliefs are challenged and exposed as prejudices

characters.name: Kant
characters.personality: depicted as a decent, stiff Tartuffe-like figure whose moralism misleads
characters.lifespan: satirized for dialectic that misguides toward categorical imperatives

characters.name: Spinoza
characters.personality: portrayed as masking philosophy with mathematical form, using 'hocus-pocus' of math to intimidate
characters.lifespan: criticized for cloaked argument and awe-inspiring form rather than candid advocacy

characters.name: Christians / Christianity
characters.personality: equated with popular Platonism for the masses; ecclesiastical oppression
characters.lifespan: portrayed as a long-standing oppressive influence that must be overcome

characters.name: The 'Will to Truth' (concept as character)
characters.personality: investigated as a driving, hazardous enterprise and questioned for its value
characters.lifespan: placed under scrutiny; its origins and value are interrogated

characters.name: Scoffers (collective)
characters.personality: mocking commentators who say dogma has fallen
characters.lifespan: exist as critics within the philosophical scene
